title: Unbounded TLV field length for string/data/list types allows OOM via crafted field
description: In loadBinaryMessage(), when a field has explicit length (string, data, list types), the field length is read from the wire as a UInt32 at line 487: fieldLength = Int(fieldLengthData.uint32!). There is no maximum bound on this value. A malicious peer can send a field with length 0xFFFFFFFF, causing data.subdata(in: offset..<offset+fieldLength) to attempt a 4 GB allocation. Even if the underlying data buffer is smaller, Swift's Data.subdata will crash with a range error rather than gracefully failing. Combined with FINDING_P_002, this is a guaranteed crash vector.
recommendation: Validate that fieldLength does not exceed the remaining data: guard offset + fieldLength <= data.count else { Logger.error("Field length exceeds message"); return }. Additionally consider a hard cap (e.g., 16 MB) for any single field.
